page: Google Cloud Platform t2d-standard-60 is a general-purpose server powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor using the x86_64 architecture. It features 60 dedicated physical cores with a single thread per core, providing 60 vCPUs. The system is equipped with 240 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced allocation of 4 GB of RAM per core. There are no bundled GPUs or local storage drives included with this instance, and it does not provide complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

In benchmark testing, the t2d-standard-60 demonstrates top-tier multi-core CPU performance, highlighted by a stress-ng multi-core score of 141,982.77 and a multi-threaded bzip3 decompression speed of 520.43 GB/sec. Single-core performance is strong, with a stress-ng single-core score of 2,693.79. Memory performance is mixed; while cached small-block read bandwidth is strong at 6,846.14 GB/sec, uncached large-block read and write speeds fall into the average tier at 141.75 GB/sec and 64.61 GB/sec respectively. The server also excels in prompt processing for small and medium language models, though text generation for large 70B models is weak at 2.56 tokens/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the t2d-standard-60 offers high resource density and strong multi-threaded compute capabilities within a standard general-purpose hardware class, making it a viable alternative to specialized compute-optimized instances for parallel workloads. However, the lack of local NVMe storage requires reliance on network-attached storage, which may impact I/O-heavy operations. This server is highly suited for multi-threaded applications, software compilation, database operations, redis caching, and medium-scale LLM prompt processing.
description: Google Cloud Platform t2d-standard-60 is a general-purpose server featuring 60 dedicated physical cores on an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor with x86_64 architecture. It provides 240 GB of RAM, maintaining a balanced 4 GB per core ratio, but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmarks show top-tier multi-core performance, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 141,982.77 and strong multi-threaded decompression capabilities. While cached memory bandwidth is strong, uncached large-block memory operations and large-model LLM text generation are average to weak. This server offers high resource density for parallel processing without the premium of specialized compute instances. It is well-suited for software compilation, database workloads, redis caching, and multi-threaded enterprise applications.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform t2d-standard-60 is a general-purpose server with 60 dedicated AMD EPYC cores and 240 GB RAM, delivering top-tier multi-core performance for parallel workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform t2d-standard-60 features 60 dedicated AMD EPYC cores and 240 GB RAM, offering strong multi-core performance for enterprise workloads.
tagline: A dedicated 60-core AMD EPYC general-purpose server delivering strong multi-threaded performance and balanced memory allocation for enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 60 dedicated AMD EPYC 7B13 physical cores with x86_64 architecture
240 GB of system memory providing 4 GB of RAM per core
Top-tier multi-core CPU and multi-threaded decompression performance
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Suited for software compilation, database operations, and redis caching
